label: Phase 3 recommendation

fieldResults.truthValue: Front-load the strongest surreal/action imagery and strip out the generic trailer setup, because the biggest retention leak is the opening and any later exposition/title-card stretches. The edit already proves it can hold attention when it delivers rapid visual novelty, so the goal is to re-cut around those peaks and compress the corporate CTA material at the end. Keep the emotional profile as high-intensity, but create clearer spike-and-release pacing so the second half feels escalatory instead of long.

fieldResults.truthValue: The weakest point is the first 0-5 seconds: patience is at its lowest there (0.2) while boredom is high (0.8), driven by the glitchy 'RISING TENSIONS' style setup that reads as generic. Retention improves immediately at 5-10 seconds, where excitement hits its peak (0.9) and boredom drops to its minimum (0.2), showing the audience responds to the city-bending, reality-breaking spectacle much more than exposition. Across the full piece, excitement stays strong on average (0.782) and remains stable, but boredom rises materially in the second half from 0.307 to 0.55, which suggests fatigue from repeated title cards, dialogue beats, and end-slate information. Specific mid/late dips align with title/exposition moments like 25-35 seconds and 85-95 seconds, where talking-head or location-card structure softens momentum. The final CTA section also hurts emotional finish: excitement bottoms out at 120 seconds (0.3) and boredom jumps again on the pre-order screens. With a friction index of 100, the cut is asking viewers to work too hard through promo structure despite having many genuinely high-performing action beats.
